target: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

findings.finding: PostgreSQL schema is already available and compatible with Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
findings.remediation: Use native PostgreSQL migration with pg_dump and pg_restore; no SSMA conversion is required.
Target PostgreSQL uses TEXT. If case-insensitive search must be preserved at the database layer, add citext or a lower(last_name) functional index.

findings.finding: H2 drop-table syntax and embedded identity behavior are not target deployment patterns.
findings.remediation: Use PostgreSQL DDL with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS and GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY.

findings.finding: MySQL schema uses AUTO_INCREMENT, UNSIGNED integer display widths and engine=InnoDB.
findings.remediation: PostgreSQL target maps identity columns to GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY, removes UNSIGNED/display widths and omits storage-engine syntax.

findings.finding: PetClinic schema has seven application tables: vets, specialties, vet_specialties, types, owners, pets and visits.
findings.remediation: Validate row counts, checksums, FK relationships and identity sequence values after restore.
